title_en: Taylor Lorenz’s Screen Time Is Almost 17 Hours a Day. 17!!!
title_zh: 泰勒·洛伦兹的每日“看屏时间”几乎有17小时。17！！！

paragraphs.en: Taylor Lorenz doesn’t want to touch grass. The technology and culture journalist wrote the bestselling book Extremely Online: The Untold Story of Fame, Influence, and Power on the Internet, and she has the screen time to prove it. Previously a reporter for mainstream media outlets, Lorenz built such a rabid following (and a legion of haters) that she decided to go independent in 2024. Now, almost 100,000 fans subscribe to her newsletter, User Mag, for her insider knowledge of virality and her freakish ability to contextualize how internet culture shapes, well, everything.
paragraphs.zh: 泰勒·洛伦兹（Taylor Lorenz）不想碰草地。她是一个科技与文化记者，写过畅销书《Extremely Online：网络世界里的名声、影响力与权力未解之谜》，并且她的屏幕时间足以证明这点。她曾是主流媒体的记者，如今在2024年选择了独立。如今，几乎有10万名读者订阅了她的通讯《User Mag》，因为她对“病毒式传播”了如指掌，而且她几乎能把“互联网文化如何塑造——嗯，几乎所有事物”这件事放在语境里说得清清楚楚。

paragraphs.en: For our very first User Behavior, Lorenz defends her extreme digital diet, revealing how she’s first to the AI slop, what it takes to run a meme page, and why she’s not worried about any of this rotting her brain.
paragraphs.zh: 在我们首期《用户行为》栏目中，洛伦兹为她极端的数字饮食辩护，透露了她为何总是最先接触“AI垃圾内容”，运营一个梗图账号需要什么，以及她为何并不担心这些内容会“腐蚀”大脑。

paragraphs.en: A regular series that quantifies the tech lifestyles of the rich and/or famous.
paragraphs.zh: 一档定期栏目，量化富人和／或名人的科技生活方式。

paragraphs.en: I’m waiting for a decent upgrade. I'll upgrade when I get a better camera. I'm friends with an influencer marketing person at Apple, and I've never gotten a single free product out of it. It's a disappointment. I'm not big-time enough to make it on the gifting!
paragraphs.zh: 我在等一部像样的升级。我会在拿到更好的摄像头时升级。我在苹果认识一位做网红营销的人，但我从未因此拿到任何免费产品。这真的让人失望。我还不够有名，没资格上他们的送礼名单！

paragraphs.en: Standard. Good computer. When I was quitting my job, I thought, what's the closest to my work computer? And I bought that. Also, I edit video. So I need a little more horsepower.
paragraphs.zh: 普通的。性能不错的电脑。我辞职时想了想，什么最接近我以前的工作电脑？于是我就买了那款。另外，我还要剪辑视频，所以需要一点更强的处理能力。

paragraphs.en: My iPhone screen time is around 10 hours or something. I am sort of horrified, but the thing is, for some reason, writing is less stressful to me on my phone. So I often write the first draft of everything on my phone. I am using it for work; I'm not just on brain-rot Instagram Reels.
paragraphs.zh: 我的 iPhone 屏幕使用时间大约是10小时左右。我有点震惊，但问题在于，出于某种原因，我在手机上写作反而压力更小。所以我经常先在手机上写好所有内容的初稿。我是在用它工作，而不是只刷那种“智力流失”的 Instagram Reels。

paragraphs.en: I also like to listen to background noise all day, because I hate the silence of downtown LA. I just always have music on. And so my two most used apps are Spotify and YouTube. At night, I like to watch Netflix on my phone because I'm still on my family plan and I can only add so many devices.
paragraphs.zh: 我还喜欢整天听环境音，因为我讨厌洛杉矶市中心的寂静。我几乎总是开着音乐。因此我最常用的两个应用是 Spotify 和 YouTube。到了晚上我也喜欢在手机上看 Netflix，因为我还在用家庭套餐，能绑定的设备数量有限。

paragraphs.en: I think caring about screen time is stupid. It's a waste of mental energy. I actually think this whole moral panic over screen time is going to be gone in 10 years, because everything's going to be integrated into the world around us already. If you're talking to an AI agent all day and just telling it what to do and interacting with it, you're not going to have to look at a screen. You'll probably just be speaking to some pod in your ear.
paragraphs.zh: 我觉得在意屏幕时间很愚蠢，浪费的是心理能量。我其实认为这场关于屏幕时间的道德恐慌十年后就会消失，因为一切都会被整合进我们周围的世界。如果你一整天都在和人工智能代理对话，只要告诉它该做什么并与它互动，你就不一定需要盯着屏幕了。你可能只是和耳边的一只“耳挂设备”说话。

paragraphs.en: I think I’m one of the last remaining users of Hype Machine, which was really popular during the bloghouse era. It started in 2005 as a way to discover MP3 blogs. I think I started using it in 2010, and I feel like I discover lots of new music on there and also they have remixes and stuff. It's honestly the best, and everybody needs to support them. I'm constantly concerned about them going out of business, because they're just from the indie internet a long time ago.
paragraphs.zh: 我想我可能是少数仍在使用Hype Machine的人之一了。那个站点在早期博客时代曾经非常流行。它始于2005年，最初是为了发现MP3博客而做的。我想我大约是在2010年开始用的，并且我在上面总能发现很多新歌，也会有翻唱和各种混音。说实话，它真的很棒，而且每个人都应该支持它。因为它来自很久以前的独立互联网，我一直担心它可能会倒闭。

paragraphs.en: I don't like the AI playlist and the AI-ification of music recommendations. I like to be given music that I would've never found before. I like the discovery aspect of Hype Machine, because it's curated by bloggers, and there are still music bloggers out there, and they're finding stuff that I wouldn't have found otherwise.
paragraphs.zh: 我不喜欢AI播放列表和“AI化”的音乐推荐。我喜欢被推荐到那些我以前根本不会碰到的音乐。我喜欢Hype Machine的发现属性，因为它由博主们来筛选和挑选，而且现在仍然有音乐博主存在，他们会找到我平时不会发现的好东西。

paragraphs.en: I listen to podcasts 24/7 on Spotify, and I do listen to music on Spotify. I just don't discover new music there as much. I do subscribe to today's top hits. I like pop music.
paragraphs.zh: 我在Spotify上24小时/7天听播客，也在Spotify上听音乐。只是我在那里发现新音乐的机会没那么多。我会订阅当下热门榜单。我喜欢流行音乐。

paragraphs.en: I don't believe in Inbox Zero. You kind of just have to treat email as a newsfeed or a Twitter feed—you're going to miss some stuff, but you can't worry about it. You just have to let it go and check in on it sometimes. A lot of people's stress about technology is feeling like they have to complete things or optimize, and I'm just like, whatever. If I see it, I see it. If it's really urgent, someone will find a way to reach me.
paragraphs.zh: 我不相信“收件箱清零”这种做法。你实际上只能把邮件当成新闻流或Twitter动态来看——你会错过一些内容，但也不用为此焦虑。你只要顺其自然，偶尔去看一下就行。很多人对技术的压力，来自于觉得自己必须把每件事都处理完、都要优化好，而我就想得很简单：反正随缘吧。我看到的我会看到；如果真的很紧急，总有人会想办法联系到我。

paragraphs.en: This number is very down. I was in the thousands. Because I'm in a lot of group chats, if I don't mark the group chats as read, it just becomes a really big number. But I've been moving a lot of my messages to Signal.
paragraphs.zh: 这个数字现在很低，我之前是几千。因为我在很多群聊里，如果我不把群聊标记为已读，它就会一直变成一个非常大的数字。不过我已经把大量消息搬到了 Signal。

paragraphs.en: I don't have any anxiety about any of it. Who cares? There's this whole industry built on making you check your phone and making you feel like you have to check your phone. And again, I mean, this has gotten me in trouble at work before. It's not a great attitude to have in a job situation, but now that I'm self-employed, I don't have a boss telling me to check my email. It's the only way to live. I couldn't get my work done if I was checking texts and email and stuff. It's too much. Why are we in this cult?
paragraphs.zh: 我对这些根本不焦虑。管它呢？这整个行业就是为了让你不断查看手机、让你觉得自己必须随时查看手机。并且说真的，这以前在工作时让我出过乱子。工作场景里这并不是一种好态度，但现在我做自由职业了，不用再有老板催着我看邮件。为了生存只能这样。如果我还要一边做事一边看短信和邮件，那根本做不完工作，太重负了。我们为什么要进这种“邪教”呢？

paragraphs.en: I don't do a lot of FaceTime. Some friends FaceTime me, but I'm not an outgoing FaceTime person. I mostly FaceTime to gossip. It's great for gossiping, because it's like you're there with them. Gossip is kind of intimate. You've got to FaceTime to make sure that no one's around. Because if you just call and start gossiping, that's a very risky endeavor.
paragraphs.zh: 我不怎么做 FaceTime。有些朋友会和我 FaceTime，但我不是那种爱打 FaceTime 的人。我大多是为了八卦才会 FaceTime。它很适合八卦，因为感觉像是和对方在同一空间。八卦本身很私密，所以你得用 FaceTime 确认四周没人。如果你只是直接打电话然后开始八卦，那是很冒险的。

paragraphs.en: This is so embarrassing. I Google that every day. I don't even know what that is, but it's the only thing I read in The Wall Street Journal. I don't know why it's called A-hed. They're all just entertaining stories.
paragraphs.zh: 这件事很尴尬。我每天都会去谷歌搜一搜。我甚至不知道那到底是什么，但这是我在《华尔街日报》上看到的唯一东西。我也不知道为什么它叫 A-hed。里面全都是娱乐八卦。

paragraphs.en: My AI history is all just random food assortments. I know AI is bad, blah, blah, blah, but I use it for recipes all the time. I usually use Gemini.
paragraphs.zh: 我的 AI 历史都是一些零散的食物组合。我知道 AI 很糟糕，诸如此类，但我一直在用它找食谱。我通常用的是 Gemini。

paragraphs.en: The heart is always my number one emoji. And then the other two I just kind of cycle through based on whatever I was texting. I use 🫠 a lot as a reaction to things like “LOL.” It’s also like, the world is bleak. I think I was using it in relation to Jasmine Crockett's Super Bowl ad today.
paragraphs.zh: 红心永远是我最常用的表情。然后另外两个我也会根据自己在发什么内容随便轮流用。我经常用🫠来回应像“LOL”这种内容。也可以说，现在的世界很灰暗。我想我今天把它用在了 Jasmine Crockett 的超级碗广告相关内容上。

paragraphs.en: Ugh, I’m a sicko. X is a terrible right-wing propaganda platform that Elon Musk has bastardized. I know it's a very skewed information environment. It incentivizes toxic behavior. But I love real-time information, and there just is no other platform that provides real-time information at scale that can also give you such a wide-ranging view into different communities. I'm very into tech Twitter, but I hate them all, and I want to see what everyone's up to. I want to see the latest discourse about Alex Karp's latest interview or something. If you want to follow what's going on in Silicon Valley, you’ve got to be on X.
paragraphs.zh: 唉，我真是个重度依赖者。X 是个可怕的右翼宣传平台，已被埃隆·马斯克彻底败坏了。我知道那里的信息环境非常偏颇，而且它会助长有害的行为。但我喜欢实时信息，而没有其他平台能在大规模范围内提供实时信息，同时还给你这么广泛地看到不同社群动向。 我很迷恋科技圈的 Twitter，但我又讨厌他们所有人，我还是想知道大家都在干什么。我想看人们对 Alex Karp 最新采访之类话题的最新讨论。如果你想追踪硅谷发生的一切，你就必须在 X 上。

paragraphs.en: Max Read coined calling Threads the “gas-leak social network,” and it is the perfect description. Threads is absolutely a bizarro world of some of the least media-literate people I've ever encountered. It's crazy. It's like a mix of brand content, hardcore blue MAGA, sort of liberal conspiracism, just really wild AI-generated headlines. There's so much fighting on Threads. It’s truly just a giant soup of misinformation and delusion.
paragraphs.zh: Max Read 说过把 Threads 称为“燃气泄漏社交网络”，这说得太准确了。Threads 的确是我见过的最不懂媒介素养的人汇聚在一起的一个奇特世界。太离谱了。它像一锅混合了品牌内容、激进蓝色 MAGA、以及某种自由派阴谋论色彩，再加上疯狂的 AI 生成标题的东西。Threads 上到处都是争吵。它几乎就是一大锅充斥着错误信息和幻想的浓汤。

paragraphs.en: I like the presentation. It’s a lot of news that I'm going to probably see on PopCrave, but he adds his own analysis. He is extremely good at recognizing things that are in the zeitgeist.
paragraphs.zh: 我喜欢这种呈现方式。这些新闻我大多都会在 PopCrave 上看到，但她还加入了自己的分析。我特别擅长抓住那些正处于潮流中心、最有共鸣的内容。

paragraphs.en: I love to know the drama. I think mod drama is an eternal form of content on the internet, and I love to keep tabs on what's going on. I also think this subreddit provides a good picture of the social dynamics that are happening on Reddit broadly.
paragraphs.zh: 我喜欢了解戏剧性的内容。我认为“版务/版主相关戏剧”几乎是网络上永恒的内容形式，而我很喜欢随时掌握发生了什么。我也认为这个 subreddit 能很好地反映 Reddit 上更广泛发生的社交动态。

paragraphs.en: I have these cords everywhere. Any room that I'm in, I bring it. I don't like to feel tethered. It's so humiliating to have to sit next to an outlet and charge your phone.
paragraphs.zh: 我到处都带着这些充电线。不管我在哪个房间，我都会把它们带上。我不喜欢被束缚住。坐在插座旁给手机充电这种事太尴尬了。

paragraphs.en: I just make them all the time. The only way to understand a community or trend or phenomenon, you have to get into that world. I also love Real Housewives and Bravo. And so I have a lot of accounts that just exist to follow Summer House news or Bravo, whatever. Because we don't have any sort of coherent tech policy that would give us the ability to exert control over our feeds, I feel like I just have to constantly make new burner feeds to get the information that I want and break out of what my personal algorithm would show me.
paragraphs.zh: 我几乎总是在发这些东西。要想真正理解一个社区、一个趋势或一个现象，必须进入那个世界。我也很喜欢《真名实姓的名媛们》（Real Housewives）和 Bravo。所以我会关注很多只为了追踪《Summer House》新闻或 Bravo 动态的账号。因为我们没有一套完整一致的技术政策来让我们掌控自己的信息流，我觉得自己只能不断创建新的临时账号/临时信息流，才能拿到我想要的信息，并摆脱我个人算法默认给我推送的内容。

paragraphs.en: I remember I had a rule: I would never date anyone that doesn't have BBM, because how am I going to communicate with them? BBM is life. And me and my best friend had both got Blackberry Pearls at the same time, and it was everything to me. I remember getting convinced to get an iPhone finally, and I was like, “God, I'm going to have to give up BBM.” And then I got the iPhone and totally never looked back. But I also loved the keyboard of it all.
paragraphs.zh: 我记得自己曾有一条原则：我不会去约会任何没有装BBM的人，因为我怎么和他们沟通呢？BBM就是生活的一部分。我的好朋友和我同时买了两部BlackBerry Pearl，那段时间对我来说意义非凡。我记得后来我终于被说服去买了iPhone，那时我想：‘天啊，我得放弃BBM了。’后来我拿到iPhone后，真的一脚把它抛在了脑后。但我也很喜欢它那副键盘。

paragraphs.en: I had many Tumblrs. This is how I got into media. I was really big on the single-serving Tumblrs. There was a whole trend: "Fuck yeah, bagels.” “Fuck yeah, L Train.” Or whatever. Most of my Tumblrs were submission-based, but I just loved the discovery aspect. I deleted a lot of them, because I don't know what I was reblogging in 2011. I don't think it was problematic. It was just a weird era of internet content. I do still have “Fuck yeah, communism.”
paragraphs.zh: 我有过很多个Tumblr账号，这也是我进入媒体行业的方式。我当时特别流行“单主题”Tumblr。那时候有一个风潮：『操，太好了，贝果。』‘操，太好了，L线。’不管是什么。我的大多数Tumblr都是投稿制账号，但我最喜欢的其实是“发现”这一环节。我后来删掉了很多，因为我不知道自己在2011年到底在转什么内容。我不觉得那是有问题的，只是当时互联网内容有点怪。现在我还留着一张‘操，太好了，社会主义。’

paragraphs.en: I don't buy into any of the classic “tech hygiene” stuff. Maybe I'm just a pig or something, but the movement exists to cause people stress and anxiety. And I think you just have to relax a little bit and not think about it. But I do try to be good about privacy. I don't post certain things, but overall, just have a secure password. Everything else, don't stress about it. Everyone has our data anyway.
paragraphs.zh: 我不太认同那些所谓传统的“网络卫生”观念。也许我就是个很‘脏’的人，但我觉得这类运动就是在给人制造压力和焦虑。我认为你其实只要放松一点，别老想这件事就好。不过我确实尽量照顾好隐私。我不会发某些内容，但总体上，只要有一个安全的密码就行。其他的东西，别去为它们担心。反正每个人的数据都已经在某种程度上被掌握了。

paragraphs.en: I have a meme page, Taylor Lorenz 3.0, so my screenshots are just content that I'm going to post. I would say I take probably dozens, if not hundreds, of screenshots a day. My default bookmarking is screenshotting and faving.
paragraphs.zh: 我有一个梗图账号，叫做Taylor Lorenz 3.0，所以我发的截图都只是我准备公开发布的内容。我大概每天会截至少几十张，甚至几百张截图。我的默认书签方式，就是截图再收藏。

paragraphs.en: A few days ago I noticed that dozens (I think actually hundreds) of meme pages were all posting the exact same memes about the Melania movie. They were appearing on everything from many many cat-theme pages, to a page dedicated to Twin Towers remembrance, a Millie Bobby Brown stan account, to pages about boxing, astronomy, and scuba diving adventures. None of the posts had any ad disclosure. Also, the posts are getting terrible engagement, but the meme pages are keeping them up, which really only happens when they're getting paid. After like eight hours of digging I found the agency behind it all. Then a new TikTok Shop ecommerce content house came up on my feed, and I started looking into that.
paragraphs.zh: 几天前，我发现几十个（我想其实是几百个）梗图账号都在反复发布完全一样的梅拉尼娅电影梗图。这些内容出现在很多很多猫主题页面上，从一个专门纪念双子塔的页面，到一个米莉·博比·布朗粉丝账号，再到拳击、天文学和水肺潜水探险相关的页面，几乎无处不在。所有这些帖子都没有任何广告披露说明。与此同时，这些帖子的互动数据也很差，但这些梗图账号却持续不断地把它们留在页面上，这种情况一般只有在有广告分成时才会发生。在我追查了大约八个小时后，我找到了这一切背后的机构。随后，我的信息流里又出现了一个新的 TikTok Shop 电商内容工作室，我便开始调查它。

paragraphs.en: All day I'm like, "Wait, what's that? What's that?" It’s just fun to go down and see what's going on in whatever corner of the internet. A lot of them are about scams. "What way is this platform being manipulated or what scam is this person running?" I have severe unmedicated ADHD, and every single day I start down a dozen different rabbit holes.
paragraphs.zh: 整天我都在想：‘咦，这是什么？这是什么？’ 就是喜欢往下点，看看互联网上各种角落到底在发生什么。其中很多内容都与诈骗有关：‘这个平台被怎样操控了？这个人到底在开什么骗局？’我有重度且未服药的 ADHD，每天都会开启十几个不同的“兔子洞”去追查。

paragraphs.en: A version of this article appears in the May/June 2026 issue. Subscribe now.
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章的一个版本将刊登在2026年5/6月号。立即订阅。
